text: We are subject to complex and evolving regulatory requirements across the jurisdictions in which we operate, including data privacy laws, financial regulations, and environmental mandates that may impose significant compliance costs.

Our reliance on TSMC for the manufacture of our most advanced semiconductor chips represents a concentration risk that could materially impact our ability to fulfill customer orders if production is disrupted.
Demand for our products in China has softened materially as macroeconomic conditions deteriorate and local competitors gain market share in key product categories.
We may not have sufficient liquidity to meet our near-term obligations if capital markets deteriorate and our revolving credit facility is not renewed on acceptable terms.
Cyberattacks targeting our cloud infrastructure could expose customer data, disrupt operations, and result in regulatory penalties and reputational harm.
We are currently subject to securities class action lawsuits and regulatory investigations that, if resolved adversely, could result in material financial penalties or injunctive relief.
The credit quality of our loan portfolio may deteriorate in a rising interest rate environment as borrowers face higher debt service obligations and reduced ability to refinance.
Escalating geopolitical tensions between the United States and China could disrupt our global supply chain, restrict market access, and trigger export control compliance obligations.
Changes to the FDA approval process and evolving drug pricing legislation could limit our revenue potential for pipeline products and reduce margins on existing therapies.
We source certain critical raw materials from a limited number of suppliers in politically unstable regions, creating potential bottlenecks that could halt manufacturing.
Consumer spending on discretionary products has declined as inflation erodes purchasing power, reducing transaction volumes and average order values across our retail segment.
Our current cash position and projected free cash flow may be insufficient to service our long-term debt if operating performance deteriorates below current levels.
A successful breach of our enterprise systems could allow unauthorized access to proprietary algorithms, customer financial records, and employee personal information.
We face potential antitrust scrutiny from the DOJ and European Commission related to our acquisition strategy and market dominance in certain software verticals.
Our commercial real estate loan book has significant exposure to office properties where occupancy rates remain below pre-pandemic levels, increasing default risk.
The ongoing conflict in Eastern Europe has disrupted energy markets, increased input costs, and created uncertainty for our European manufacturing operations.
New SEC disclosure requirements for climate-related financial risks will require significant investment in data collection infrastructure and third-party auditing.
We depend on a single logistics partner for last-mile delivery in Southeast Asia. Any service disruption could delay order fulfillment and damage customer relationships.
Subscriber growth in our streaming business has plateaued as the market reaches saturation and competition from new entrants intensifies, pressuring revenue forecasts.
If the credit facility covenant relating to our interest coverage ratio is breached, lenders may accelerate repayment, creating an immediate liquidity shortfall.
State-sponsored actors have repeatedly attempted to infiltrate our research and development networks to steal intellectual property and competitive intelligence.
Ongoing patent infringement litigation filed by a competitor could result in injunctions restricting our product sales and royalty payments that materially affect profitability.
Rising delinquency rates on our consumer credit card portfolio indicate that lower-income borrowers are experiencing financial stress, which may increase charge-off rates.
Trade policy changes, including new tariffs on semiconductor imports, could increase our manufacturing costs and disrupt the global market equilibrium for our products.
The GDPR and California Consumer Privacy Act impose strict obligations on how we collect, store, and process personal data, with fines of up to 4% of global annual revenue for non-compliance.
